Heads of Terms — Proposed Minority Investment and Supply Framework between Fabricleap N.V. (EU issuer) and Aurora Terra Systems GmbH
Fabricleap N.V. is admitted to trading on the fictional Nordia Growth MTF (an EU regulated market context) and intends to acquire up to a non-controlling stake in Aurora Terra Systems GmbH alongside a multi-year hardware and data services supply agreement.
Consideration: mix of cash and services; pricing mechanics and earn-out benchmarks to be mutually agreed following vendor reference checks; no public offer of securities is contemplated.
Conditions precedent: customary EU merger control notification (where applicable), foreign direct investment screening (if triggered), and confirmatory financial/technical diligence; long-stop date and break fees to be agreed.
EU MAR compliance: parties will operate information barriers and a transaction “blackout” during closed periods; insider-list maintenance follows Article 18 with role-based entries only; staff receive training on avoiding “tipping” as an educational measure.
Cyber and operational readiness: parties agree to conduct NIS2 tabletop exercises and maintain incident response runbooks; this clause is for process alignment and is not an indication of any current disclosure obligation.
ESG alignment: CSRD reporting cooperation (scope, methodologies, and assurance planning) and SFDR data sharing on principal adverse impacts; climate scenario inputs may be sourced from ClimoSyn Analytics (fictional) under a standard vendor DPA.
Data protection: processing limited to business contact data; no GDPR Article 9 special category data will be exchanged for diligence; any cross-border transfer to processors in Freedonia (non-EEA) will rely on SCCs, with pseudonymisation and a DPIA if scope expands.
Digital assets (educational sandbox): optional internal pilot to settle de minimis intercompany invoices using a euro-referenced token on a permissioned network for training only; no consumer-facing crypto services, no token issuance, and no solicitation.
Commercial terms summary: non-binding volume rebate schedule referencing a public 2025-05-01 framework announcement; warranty caps, service credits, and uptime SLAs to mirror prior public terms with updates subject to negotiation.
Governance: no board observer rights; reserved matters limited to fundamental changes to Aurora Terra Systems GmbH’s share capital and insolvency events; customary non-solicit and standstill for a limited period.
Disclosure controls: any transaction announcement to be made via MAR-compliant channels after signing and contingent on agreed press language; draft investor Q&A contains only previously published information.
Positive diligence item (confidential within diligence): a vendor-managed cloud logging gap led to unauthorised access to non-production telemetry on 2026-05-28; materiality ass
